title: digitalis purpurea var. nevadensis
intro: Digitalis purpurea var. nevadensis is a subspecies of the foxglove plant, known for its tall spikes of purple flowers and native to the western United States. The plant contains digoxin, a cardiac glycoside with medicinal properties that have been used to treat various heart conditions, including atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Digitalis purpurea var. nevadensis include its ability to increase cardiac output, reduce symptoms of heart failure, and improve exercise tolerance. Digitalis purpurea var. nevadensis is typically prepared for medicinal use through the extraction of its leaves, which can be made into tinctures, capsules, or other herbal preparations.
